subject: Sociology
title: Credentialism
description: A quick, practical guide to Credentialism.

hook.text: Jobs that once needed a high school diploma now require a bachelor's. The work didn't change — the credential did.

buildup.text: Credentialism is the growing trend of requiring degrees for jobs that don't actually need them.

discovery.text: Randall Collins argued that credentials serve as gatekeeping tools, not proof of job-relevant knowledge.

twist.text: Credential inflation benefits universities and excludes capable people who can't afford or access degrees.

climax.text: Degrees often signal class status, not competence. Credentialism turns education into a sorting machine.

punchline.text: The diploma matters more than the skill.

quiz.question: What is credentialism?
options: Requiring increasingly higher credentials for jobs
